response_shape.access_token: JWT string (HS256). Decoded payload: nameid, unique_name, company_id, user_root, partner_id, is_head_office, session_id, nbf, exp, iat
response_shape.user_info: { id, name, userName, partnerId, phone, email, avatar, companyId }
response_shape.session_info: fat object: id, name, userName, userPartnerId, partnerId, userCompanies (currentCompany + allowedCompanies[]), expirationDate, isAdmin, permissions[], groups[], rules[], modules[], settings, expiredIn (seconds), tenantId, isUppercasePartnerName, features[], totpEnabled, configs[]

additional_headers_observed.X-Session-Id: from localStorage['X-Session-Id'], same as session_id claim in JWT

notes: Tested with admin/123123@. POST /api/Account/Login returns the JSON above. Bearer token alone is enough for every /api/* endpoint tested; X-Session-Id and X-Device-Id are not strictly required but always sent by the SPA. No captcha, no 2FA (totpEnabled=false). The exact POST body shape was not directly captured (Playwright MCP does not surface request bodies); confirm via a quick Playwright login script on first build run.
